zsh.h(3) Library Functions Manual zsh.h(3)

NAME

zsh.h - Zsh shell completion generator.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE *output)
Generate zsh completion script.

Detailed Description

Zsh shell completion generator.

Definition in file zsh.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE * output)

Generate zsh completion script.

Definition at line 209 of file zsh.c.

209                                                          {
210   if (!output) {
211     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Output stream cannot be NULL");
212   }
213 
214   /* Load binary-level and discovery options for binary-level completion.
215    * Binary-level completion (options before mode name like --color-mode) uses
216    * _arguments with -n -S flags to establish proper zsh completion context.
217    * Without _arguments, completion functions fail with "can only be called from
218    * completion function" error, causing zsh to display "corrections (errors: N)"
219    * messages. The -n flag disables defaults and -S disables short option
220    * processing, allowing safe context establishment. */
221   size_t binary_count = 0;
222   const option_descriptor_t *binary_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, true, &binary_count);
223 
224   size_t discovery_count = 0;
225   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, false, &discovery_count);
226 
227   fprintf(output, "# Zsh completion script for ascii-chat\n"
228                   "# Generated from options registry - DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\n"
229                   "\n"
230                   "# Configure completion for ascii-chat\n"
231                   "# Use only _complete (no corrections or approximate matching)\n"
232                   "zstyle ':completion:*:ascii-chat:*' completer _complete\n"
233                   "\n"
234                   "# Device index completion helpers\n"
235                   "_ascii_chat_webcam_indices() {\n"
236                   "  local -a indices\n"
237                   "  local output\n"
238                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-webcams 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
239                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
240                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
241                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
242                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
243                   "  _describe 'webcam' indices\n"
244                   "}\n"
245                   "\n"
246                   "_ascii_chat_microphone_indices() {\n"
247                   "  local -a indices\n"
248                   "  local output\n"
249                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-microphones 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
250                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
251                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
252                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
253                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
254                   "  _describe 'microphone' indices\n"
255                   "}\n"
256                   "\n"
257                   "_ascii_chat_speakers_indices() {\n"
258                   "  local -a indices\n"
259                   "  local output\n"
260                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-speakers 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
261                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
262                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
263                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
264                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
265                   "  _describe 'speakers' indices\n"
266                   "}\n"
267                   "\n"
268                   "_ascii_chat_binary() {\n"
269                   "  local -a binary_opts=(\n");
270 
271   /* Add discovery options to binary-level completion */
272   if (discovery_opts) {
273     for (size_t i = 0; i < discovery_count; i++) {
274       fprintf(output, "    '--%s:", discovery_opts[i].long_name);
275       zsh_escape_desc(output, discovery_opts[i].help_text);
276       fprintf(output, "'\n");
277     }
278   }
279 
280   fprintf(output, "  )\n"
281                   "  _values 'option' \"${binary_opts[@]}\"\n"
282                   "}\n"
283                   "\n"
284                   "_ascii_chat_binary_grouped() {\n"
285                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
286                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
287                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
288 
289   /* Write binary-level options in grouped format */
290   if (binary_opts) {
291     zsh_write_combined_args(output, binary_opts, binary_count);
292   }
293 
294   fprintf(output, "}\n"
295                   "\n"
296                   "_ascii_chat() {\n"
297                   "  if [[ ${words[2]} == -* ]]; then\n"
298                   "    # Binary-level options: show both binary and discovery mode options\n"
299                   "    # (since discovery is the default mode that runs at binary level)\n"
300                   "    _ascii_chat_binary_grouped\n"
301                   "    _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
302                   "    return\n"
303                   "  fi\n"
304                   "\n"
305                   "  # Mode routing: check words[2] to determine which mode function to call\n"
306                   "  case $words[2] in\n"
307                   "    # Server-like modes\n"
308                   "    server)\n"
309                   "      _ascii_chat_server\n"
310                   "      return\n"
311                   "      ;;\n"
312                   "    discovery-service)\n"
313                   "      _ascii_chat_discovery_service\n"
314                   "      return\n"
315                   "      ;;\n"
316                   "\n"
317                   "    # Client-like modes\n"
318                   "    client)\n"
319                   "      _ascii_chat_client\n"
320                   "      return\n"
321                   "      ;;\n"
322                   "    mirror)\n"
323                   "      _ascii_chat_mirror\n"
324                   "      return\n"
325                   "      ;;\n"
326                   "    # Default/unknown input - show mode selection only if truly empty, otherwise discovery\n"
327                   "    *)\n"
328                   "      # If words[2] is empty AND we're at position 2, show mode selection\n"
329                   "      # Otherwise (even for partial input like 'disc'), use discovery mode (the default)\n"
330                   "      if [[ -z \"${words[2]}\" && $CURRENT -eq 2 ]]; then\n"
331                   "        # Completing the mode name itself - show available modes\n"
332                   "        local -a server_modes client_modes\n"
333                   "        server_modes=(\n");
334 
335   /* Generate server-like modes from registry */
336   size_t mode_server_count = 0;
337   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_server_descs = get_modes_by_group("server-like", &mode_server_count);
338   if (mode_server_descs) {
339     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_server_count; i++) {
340       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_server_descs[i].name, mode_server_descs[i].description);
341     }
342     SAFE_FREE(mode_server_descs);
343   }
344 
345   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
346                   "        client_modes=(\n");
347 
348   /* Generate client-like modes from registry (exclude discovery since it's the default mode) */
349   size_t mode_client_count = 0;
350   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_client_descs = get_modes_by_group("client-like", &mode_client_count);
351   if (mode_client_descs) {
352     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_client_count; i++) {
353       /* Skip discovery mode - it's the default when no mode is specified */
354       if (strcmp(mode_client_descs[i].name, "discovery") == 0) {
355         continue;
356       }
357       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_client_descs[i].name, mode_client_descs[i].description);
358     }
359     SAFE_FREE(mode_client_descs);
360   }
361 
362   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
363                   "        _describe -t server-modes 'server-like modes' server_modes\n"
364                   "        _describe -t client-modes 'client-like modes' client_modes\n"
365                   "      else\n"
366                   "        # Default mode (discovery) - complete its options\n"
367                   "        _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
368                   "      fi\n"
369                   "      ;;\n"
370                   "  esac\n"
371                   "}\n"
372                   "\n"
373                   "# Server-like modes: handle incoming connections and stream management\n"
374                   "_ascii_chat_server() {\n"
375                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
376                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
377                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
378 
379   /* Server options - grouped by category */
380   size_t server_count = 0;
381   const option_descriptor_t *server_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_SERVER, false, &server_count);
382 
383   if (server_opts) {
384     zsh_write_combined_args(output, server_opts, server_count);
385     SAFE_FREE(server_opts);
386   }
387 
388   fprintf(output, "}\n"
389                   "\n"
390                   "_ascii_chat_discovery_service() {\n"
391                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
392                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
393                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
394 
395   /* Discovery-service options - grouped by category */
396   size_t discovery_svc_count = 0;
397   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_svc_opts =
398       options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, false, &discovery_svc_count);
399 
400   if (discovery_svc_opts) {
401     zsh_write_combined_args(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count);
402     SAFE_FREE(discovery_svc_opts);
403   }
404 
405   fprintf(output, "}\n"
406                   "\n"
407                   "# Client-like modes: connect to servers or render local media\n"
408                   "_ascii_chat_client() {\n"
409                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
410                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
411                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
412 
413   /* Client options - grouped by category */
414   size_t client_count = 0;
415   const option_descriptor_t *client_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_CLIENT, false, &client_count);
416 
417   if (client_opts) {
418     zsh_write_combined_args(output, client_opts, client_count);
419     SAFE_FREE(client_opts);
420   }
421 
422   fprintf(output, "}\n"
423                   "\n"
424                   "_ascii_chat_mirror() {\n"
425                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
426                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
427                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
428 
429   /* Mirror options - grouped by category */
430   size_t mirror_count = 0;
431   const option_descriptor_t *mirror_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_MIRROR, false, &mirror_count);
432 
433   if (mirror_opts) {
434     zsh_write_combined_args(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count);
435     SAFE_FREE(mirror_opts);
436   }
437 
438   fprintf(output, "}\n"
439                   "\n"
440                   "# Discovery mode: find sessions via discovery service (default mode when no mode specified)\n"
441                   "_ascii_chat_discovery() {\n"
442                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
443                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
444                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
445 
446   /* Discovery options - grouped by category (reuse already-loaded options) */
447   if (discovery_opts) {
448     zsh_write_combined_args(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count);
449   }
450 
451   fprintf(output, "}\n"
452                   "\n"
453                   "# Register completion functions (must come after definitions)\n"
454                   "compdef _ascii_chat ascii-chat\n"
455                   "compdef _ascii_chat 'build/bin/ascii-chat'\n");
456 
457   SAFE_FREE(binary_opts);
458   SAFE_FREE(discovery_opts);
459 
460   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
461 }










Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.4
    ascii-chat
